You search for objective explanations, there might be some, but more probable is that it is entirely his subjective experience.
I looks like he thinks it is objective and this might prevent him from finding the right approach.
In my subjective experience hunger is different from pain. I haven’t done any extensive experiments but for example I can distract myself from feeling hunger, pain much less, also hunger seems to be very dependent on the timing of the meals, if you have regular meals you feel it much more when the time is close. In general it seems that there might be more interventions than with pain. But eventually it is subjective- we cannot know what he can do about it and what he can’t.

messages.message: I would like to start doing the ORI
okay, gonna go sentence by sentence, in thread.
messages.thread: I would like to start doing the ORI

messages.message: Some theories do more than explain a class of systems. (B) They also specify distinctions to make, decisions to take, artifacts to produce, and tests to apply. (B) When a capable interpreter can act on such a theory, the theory doubles as a construction mechanism. (U-a mechanism constructing what?) An LLM is one practical interpreter path (U): natural-language methodology can produce operational behavior without first being compiled into a separate implementation language(B)—but the prose still requires human authoring and curation, and other interpreters (rule engines, human operators following runbooks) can also apply actionable theory. (B)
messages.thread: I would like to start doing the ORI

messages.message:
The relationship takes three progressively stronger forms—not necessarily strict prerequisites, but increasing demands on self-targeting and verification (U - what relationship are we talking about here?):

Executable theory. An interpreter applies a theory to construct or operate some system. (B, but new term)
Reflexive executable theory. The system carrying the theory is among the systems the theory can construct or modify. (B, but new term)
Verified reflexive extension. Candidate modifications are retained only when an applicable oracle accepts them as improvements. (U - not sure what 'oracle' means here)

messages.thread: I would like to start doing the ORI

messages.message:
What I've learned from this is that this is very piece by piece. Thank you.

I am now working on splitting the article into individual notes - but maybe I'll then regenerate the synthesis back.  I'll also try to to add explanations to the points you marked as U.
messages.thread: I would like to start doing the ORI

messages.message: i can see there is a lot of really useful and important stuff here - it's difficult because i may not be your intended audience. Much of which is a U for me, a guy who is only just dipping his toe into AI having been skeptical until now, will be an A or a B for someone who, like you, has operationalized these concepts.
messages.thread: I would like to start doing the ORI

messages.message: Some of it may use conventions which i'm not aware of. But in doing this A/B/U evaluation - in order for it to be valuable in an important way, i think we should have a norm of explaining our background/B floor - ideally in the 'u notes' (notes from U? notes for U? workshopping a pithy term here lol)
messages.thread: I would like to start doing the ORI

messages.message: Something i've come across also - in a totally different arena - is when an article will have a 'technical section' which explains the nitty gritty reasoning for SME's, and also a section of 'the results/what you can take from this'

messages.thread: I would like to start doing the ORI

messages.message: quick looking over the rest before sleep:
messages.thread: I would like to start doing the ORI

messages.message: 'theory becomes system machinery when four conditions coincide' - my thought is that 'living system machinery' may be a better term because 'system' by itself doesn't imply mutability
messages.thread: I would like to start doing the ORI

messages.message: this is a big A for me: The same text may be read as evidence in one call and treated as prescription in another, but that duality is not guaranteed: it depends on prompt framing, and stochastic interpretation can diverge across sessions. The theory has not become deterministic code; at most it changes the conditions for subsequent interpretation rather than rewriting the executing machinery itself.
messages.thread: I would like to start doing the ORI

messages.message: 'Reflexivity begins where a methodology is closed under its own recommendations' is a good point
